reasoning: The yfinance tool provides a combined near-term estimate of 0.41, which is driven by negative seasonality compared to previous years and recent flat-to-negative momentum, despite a slight positive historical linear trend. As instructed, weekly stock moves are largely unpredictable, so we moderate this anchor closer to 0.5. A recent €1.3 billion debt offering on June 20 might act as a minor near-term headwind. Over longer horizons (from July to December), forecasting whether the stock will remain above a specific threshold becomes increasingly dominated by market noise. Under the efficient market hypothesis, stock price movements are close to a random walk in expected value, especially once ex-dividend drops are accounted for (O pays monthly dividends, which naturally drag on share price over time). Accordingly, the probabilities reliably converge toward 0.50 as the horizon extends.
